metrics.label: Estimated cost
metrics.status: OpenAI baseline vs Reference target budget <= $0.1000

featuredCase.title: Developer adoption friction briefing
featuredCase.sourceEvidence: Quickstart completion is improving but setup remains a drop-off point.
featuredCase.baseline: Next product-quality improvement: ship a first-class, public, self-serve setup recovery path (and validate it in the lab) before adding new pilot teams. Concretely, expose a documented recovery checklist for the top failure modes (stale CLI, missing environment variables, wrong-workspace credentials) and ensure the flow supports recovery from partial/failed installs without requiring users to restart from the beginning. This addresses the largest remaining onboarding drop and the most common support drivers, and it is the explicit gate for pilot expansion readiness.
featuredCase.candidate: Ship the recovery-path copy and trace-backed citation checks before widening the pilot.
featuredCase.evaluatorNote: Comparison uses file-backed artifacts for the same eval case so the before/after story is inspectable.

recommendation.label: Pipeline rehearsal
recommendation.text: This comparison uses OpenAI baseline and a human-authored Reference target. It validates the eval artifact flow, but not live model quality improvement.
recommendation.warning: Run a generated candidate before using this as improvement evidence.
